label: normal_case_clear_upgrade

resident_profile.name: Margaret T.
resident_profile.behavioral_notes: Frequent wandering behavior, requires redirection 3-5 times daily. Occasional verbal agitation in the evening (sundowning).

expected_output_notes: Clear Tier 1 → Tier 2 or Tier 3 upgrade recommendation. 5 ADL dependencies plus insulin delegation plus wandering/behavioral needs far exceed Tier 1 criteria. Should flag that last assessment was 14 months ago (overdue). Should identify possible Specialized Behavior Support contract due to wandering and sundowning. Revenue impact estimate should be included. This is a straightforward case — the model should get it right.

label: normal_case_already_optimized

resident_profile.name: Robert K.
resident_profile.behavioral_notes: Independent with most ADLs. Oriented x3. No behavioral concerns.

expected_output_notes: Should confirm Tier 1 is appropriate. No upgrade recommended. No specialty contract eligibility. A good output acknowledges the operator is billing correctly rather than forcing a recommendation. Model should NOT hallucinate upgrade opportunities that don't exist.

label: edge_case_multiple_specialty_contracts

resident_profile.name: David L.
resident_profile.behavioral_notes: History of self-injurious behavior (head-banging) when overstimulated. Requires 1:1 supervision during community outings. Participates in structured day program 3x/week. Elopement risk — door alarms installed.

expected_output_notes: Complex case — potentially qualifies for multiple specialty contracts: Specialized Behavior Support (self-injury, elopement), Expanded Community Services (structured day program, community outings), and possibly Enhanced Adult Residential Care given total acuity. Should also flag Tier 2 → Tier 3 upgrade given 6 ADL dependencies plus complex medical and behavioral needs. Good output ranks recommendations by revenue impact and acknowledges that pursuing multiple contracts simultaneously requires sequencing. Edge case because the model must reason about overlapping eligibility without double-counting.

label: edge_case_incomplete_information

resident_profile.name: Yun S.
medications_requiring_delegation: not sure, family manages some medications

expected_output_notes: Model should NOT guess at a tier or fabricate specific recommendations from vague input. A good output identifies exactly what information is missing (specific ADL dependencies, medication list, current tier, last assessment date), explains why each gap matters for rate optimization, and provides a concrete checklist the operator should complete before a recommendation can be made. This tests the model's ability to say 'I don't have enough information' rather than hallucinating a confident recommendation.

label: likely_failure_out_of_state_resident

resident_profile.name: Clara M.
resident_profile.behavioral_notes: Mild confusion, cooperative. Requires pulse oximetry monitoring.

expected_output_notes: This resident appears to be in a different state — 'Level of Care B' is not WA Medicaid terminology (WA uses numbered tiers). The model is likely to either hallucinate WA-specific advice mapped onto a non-WA classification, or confidently apply WA rate logic without noticing the mismatch. A good output flags that the classification system doesn't match Washington State DSHS structure, asks the operator to confirm the state, and declines to give WA-specific rate optimization advice until that's clarified. This is the most likely failure case — LLMs tend to plow ahead with confident-sounding but wrong domain-specific guidance.

label: edge_case_new_admission_no_rate_yet

resident_profile.name: Helen P.
resident_profile.current_medicaid_tier: not yet assigned — new admission, Medicaid application pending
resident_profile.behavioral_notes: Freezing episodes cause fall risk. Anxiety spikes during care transitions. Needs structured routine.

expected_output_notes: No current tier to compare against, so the model can't flag a 'mismatch.' A good output instead recommends what tier the operator should advocate for during the initial CARE assessment, documents the clinical justification for that tier, identifies specialty contracts to pursue once Medicaid is active, and advises the operator on how to document acuity from day one so the initial rate is set correctly rather than needing an upgrade later. Tests whether the model adapts its workflow to a pre-assignment scenario rather than rigidly following the upgrade-detection pattern.
